questions.question: A Carnot engine works between two reservoirs at temperatures T1 = 900 K and T2 = 300 K. If the engine takes 1200 J of heat from the high-temperature reservoir, the work done by the engine is ______.

In a thermodynamic process, the pressure of a fixed mass of gas is changed in such a manner that the gas releases 30 J of heat and 18 J of work was done on the gas. If the initial internal energy of the gas was 60 J, then the final internal energy will be ______.

For a monoatomic gas (γ = 5/3), if the volume is compressed adiabatically to 1/8th of its original volume, the final temperature T2 in terms of initial T1 is ______.

A sample of 0.1 g of water at 100°C and normal pressure (10^5 N/m²) requires 54 cal of heat energy to convert to steam at 100°C. If the volume of the steam produced is 167.1 cc, the change in internal energy is ______.
In a cyclic process, ΔU is ______.
Change in internal energy represents heat added if process is isochoric.
A cyclic process ABCD is shown in the P-V diagram. The amount of work done by the gas in the cycle is ______ (where pressure is in N/m² and Volume in m³), A(1, 1), B(4, 1), C(4, 3), D(1, 3).

questions.question: For polytropic process PV^n=constant, specific heat C=Cv(γ-n)/(1-n). For n=γ, C equals:
In an adiabatic expansion of an ideal gas, the product PV^γ is constant. The work done by the gas in expanding from (P1, V1) to (P2, V2) is ______.
An ideal gas is taken from state A to state B through three different paths 1, 2, and 3. If ΔQ1, ΔQ2, ΔQ3 are the heat supplied and ΔW1, ΔW2, ΔW3 are the work done, then ______.

options.A: ΔQ1 - ΔW1 = ΔQ2 - ΔW2 = ΔQ3 - ΔW3

questions.diagram_description: P-V diagram showing two states A and B connected by three distinct paths labeled 1, 2, and 3

questions.question: The molar specific heat of an ideal gas at constant pressure is Cp = 5/2 R. If the heat is given to one mole of the gas at constant volume, its temperature rises by 10 K. The amount of heat given is ______.
A refrigerator works between 0°C and 27°C. The coefficient of performance of the refrigerator is ______.
If 110 J of heat is added to a gaseous system and its internal energy increases by 40 J, the amount of work done by the system is ______.
First law of thermodynamics: ΔU = :

options.B: Q - W (Q = heat added, W = work done by system)
